findings.title: Structure: CG-BGID-001 deviates from its compliance-gap template
findings.detail: “Remediation” long at 54 words (template 8–40)

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-001
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-001 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-002
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-002 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-003
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-003 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-004
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-004 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-005
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-005 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-006
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-006 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: PS-BGID-006 (Guarantee Generation and Delivery) has no control linked
findings.detail: No control element points to PS-BGID-006 via its step field. This is the step that generates the legal guarantee instrument, transmits it via SWIFT MT760, and updates the client facility utilisation — yet it is the only critical issuance step with no preventive or detective control attached.

findings.title: PS-BGID-003 (Wording Review) has no control linked
findings.detail: No control element links to PS-BGID-003. The step handles approval of bespoke legal guarantee wording — a legally consequential activity — against only an informal 2-business-day desk convention that is not tracked in TFS and carries no escalation trigger. All five controls (CP-BGID-001 through CP-BGID-005) point to other steps; none covers the wording review step.

findings.title: PS-BGID-002 (Credit and Facility Check) has no Accountable entry in any role RACI
findings.detail: ROLE-BGID-001 (Trade Finance Officer) records PS-BGID-002:R but not PS-BGID-002:A. No other role element includes an :A entry for PS-BGID-002. The step owner field says Trade Finance Officer, which implies accountability, but the RACI as written leaves PS-BGID-002 without a formally accountable party.

findings.title: PS-BGID-003 RACI contradicts the step stated accountability
findings.detail: PS-BGID-003 declares owner: Trade Finance Officer and its body states the TFO remains accountable throughout with Legal acting as consulted. However, ROLE-BGID-001 records PS-BGID-003:I (Informed only), while ROLE-BGID-006 records PS-BGID-003:R and PS-BGID-003:A. The step body and RACI entries directly contradict each other on which party is accountable.

findings.title: JT-BGID-003 says wording review wait is undefined, contradicting the 2-BD SLA on PS-BGID-003
findings.detail: PS-BGID-003 records sla: '2 business days (bespoke wording — informal desk convention; not tracked in TFS)'. JT-BGID-003, which occurs at the same step, states the wait is undefined and no interim status updates are provided. If a desk SLA exists — even informally — it should be reflected in the touchpoint rather than described as undefined.

findings.title: PS-BGID-006 has no step-level SLA despite a client-facing delivery touchpoint (JT-BGID-005)
findings.detail: JT-BGID-005 (MT760 Issuance Confirmation) sets a concrete client expectation. EX-BGID-001 references 'the 3-business-day SLA' as a threshold that SWIFT failure can breach. However, M-BGID-001 defines that as an end-to-end metric, and PS-BGID-006 records an empty sla field. Is there a step-level turnaround target for guarantee generation and delivery?

findings.title: Head of Trade Finance RACI is C (Consulted) but role and control require mandatory approval above EUR 5M
findings.detail: CP-BGID-001 states the Head of Trade Finance must also provide sign-off before issuance proceeds for guarantees above EUR 5M, and PS-BGID-005 confirms this is system-enforced. Yet the RACI entry for ROLE-BGID-003 at PS-BGID-005 is C (Consulted), not A or R. Consulted means the role is asked for input but carries no accountability or execution duty — directly contradicting the stated mandatory approval obligation.

findings.title: No control covers the re-screening obligation cited in EX-BGID-005 (post-rejection wording amendment)
findings.detail: EX-BGID-005 states that when a beneficiary bank rejects the MT760 wording and an amended guarantee is re-issued, a second sanctions screening is triggered. Neither CP-BGID-002 nor CP-BGID-005 is linked to this re-issuance path — both are bound only to PS-BGID-004 in the normal flow. No control or step covers sanctions screening of the amended instrument before the replacement MT760 is transmitted.

findings.title: CP-BGID-003 (Facility Limit Check) does not cover the intraday double-issuance exposure in EX-BGID-006
findings.detail: EX-BGID-006 documents an intraday window of up to one business day during which a completed issuance is not yet reflected in the facility system. A same-day second application could pass CP-BGID-003 against headroom already consumed. The described mitigation is a manual TFO check, which is not documented as a formal control — it has no owner, no trigger definition and no audit trail requirement.

findings.title: REG-BGID-004 (EBA ML/TF Risk Factors) compliance coverage is unresolved
findings.detail: REG-BGID-004's How it is met section states that broader ML/TF risk assessment for individual guarantee applications is to be confirmed with the SME. CP-BGID-002 addresses only the beneficiary sanctions check. EBA GL/2023-03 requires a product-specific risk assessment covering country risk, counterparty risk and product risk. No control covers this broader assessment.

findings.title: REG-BGID-011 and REG-BGID-012 have no linked control and contain unresolved to-be-confirmed gaps
findings.detail: REG-BGID-011 states machine-readable retention and the 5-year purge schedule are to be confirmed with Compliance and Records Management. REG-BGID-012 states COREP C07.00 population from the TFS feed is to be confirmed with Finance and Regulatory Reporting. Neither regulation is backed by a named control in the wiki.

findings.title: FP-BGID-001 and FP-BGID-002 describe the same client experience without a link between them
findings.detail: Both friction-points occur at PS-BGID-003, both point to PP-BGID-002, and both describe the same root cause: Legal reviews bespoke wording without a committed SLA and without client-facing status updates. FP-BGID-001 frames this as no committed turnaround and FP-BGID-002 as no queue visibility, but the Description, Root cause and Client impact sections substantially overlap. Either one is a duplicate or they are genuinely distinct friction-points that should cross-reference each other.

findings.title: FP-BGID-003 has no painPoint link despite describing a discrete client pain
findings.detail: FP-BGID-003 (Collateral Booking Status Opaque to Client) has no painPoint field. There is no pain-point in the wiki covering this issue. II-BGID-005 addresses FP-BGID-003 directly, but the friction to pain-point traceability link is broken.

findings.title: FP-BGID-004 has no painPoint link despite describing a discrete client pain
findings.detail: FP-BGID-004 (MT760 Delivery Not Acknowledged to Client) has no painPoint field. No pain-point in the wiki covers this issue. MT-BGID-003 and JT-BGID-005 both describe the same silent gap, and II-BGID-004 and TD-BGID-001 both claim to resolve FP-BGID-004 — but without a pain-point element, the issue is absent from the pain-points section.

findings.title: MT-BGID-003 and JT-BGID-005 describe the MT760 acknowledgement gap but neither links to FP-BGID-004
findings.detail: Both MT-BGID-003 and JT-BGID-005 articulate the same experience as FP-BGID-004 (silent gap after MT760 issuance). However, neither the moment nor the touchpoint carries a reference to FP-BGID-004, and FP-BGID-004 does not reference them. The same client experience is documented independently in three element types with no cross-links.

findings.title: JT-BGID-001 and PP-BGID-003 describe the same incomplete-submission loop — are they intentionally unlinked?
findings.detail: JT-BGID-001 describes clients discovering missing fields after submission. PP-BGID-003 documents the same issue as the most frequent intake failure. Neither element references the other. It is unclear whether a link from JT-BGID-001 to PP-BGID-003 was omitted by oversight.

findings.title: II-BGID-004 claims to address FP-BGID-004 but its body does not support the link
findings.detail: II-BGID-004 (ICC-SWIFT API Application Channel for ERP intake) lists FP-BGID-004 (MT760 Delivery Not Acknowledged to Client) in its addresses field, but the idea body is entirely about eliminating incomplete intake submissions. MT760 acknowledgement notification is an issuance-delivery friction unrelated to intake; no part of the idea text explains how it resolves FP-BGID-004.

findings.title: FP-BGID-004 (MT760 delivery not acknowledged) has no innovation-idea genuinely addressing it
findings.detail: The only innovation-idea listing FP-BGID-004 in its addresses field is II-BGID-004, but that idea addresses ERP intake not post-issuance notification. No other innovation-idea addresses the MT760 acknowledgement gap, leaving this friction-point without a credible innovation response.

findings.title: FP-BGID-003 has no linked pain-point — is the collateral-opacity issue a stand-alone friction or should a pain-point exist?
findings.detail: FP-BGID-003 (Collateral Booking Status Opaque to Client) has no painPoint frontmatter field, unlike FP-BGID-001 and FP-BGID-002 which link to PP-BGID-002. It is unclear whether the collateral-opacity issue is a manifestation of an existing pain-point or is intentionally stand-alone. If a pain-point is implied, it is not documented.

findings.title: FP-BGID-004 has no linked pain-point — is the MT760 acknowledgement gap a stand-alone friction or should a pain-point exist?
findings.detail: FP-BGID-004 (MT760 Delivery Not Acknowledged to Client) has no painPoint frontmatter field. It is unclear whether the MT760 acknowledgement gap is a manifestation of a broader pain-point or is intentionally recorded as a stand-alone friction. If a pain-point is implied, it is not documented.

findings.title: SYS-BGID-005 and INT-BGID-004 cite PS-BGID-007 for post-issuance utilisation update but PS-BGID-007 is Collateral Confirmation
findings.detail: SYS-BGID-005 Role in this process states the Facility/Credit System is updated asynchronously post-issuance at PS-BGID-007. INT-BGID-004 likewise records the post-issuance utilisation update at PS-BGID-007. However, PS-BGID-007 is Collateral Confirmation — a pre-issuance step. The actual post-issuance facility utilisation update is described in PS-BGID-006 (Guarantee Generation and Delivery), whose outputs include Client facility utilisation updated. The step ID cited in both elements is wrong.

findings.title: SYS-BGID-005 (Facility/Credit System) missing from PS-BGID-002 systems field despite being consulted at that step
findings.detail: SYS-BGID-005 Role in this process and INT-BGID-004 state the Facility/Credit System is consulted synchronously at PS-BGID-002 (Credit and Facility Check). However, PS-BGID-002 frontmatter lists only [SYS-BGID-002] in its systems field. SYS-BGID-005 is absent, creating a gap between what the system and integration elements document and what the process step records.

findings.title: SYS-BGID-006 (Document Management System) missing from PS-BGID-006 systems field despite receiving documents at that step
findings.detail: SYS-BGID-006 Role in this process and INT-BGID-005 state the DMS receives executed guarantee documents at PS-BGID-006. However, PS-BGID-006 frontmatter lists only [SYS-BGID-002, SYS-BGID-004]. SYS-BGID-006 is absent.

findings.title: PS-BGID-007 (Collateral Confirmation) has no systems linked despite recording a collateral block confirmation
findings.detail: PS-BGID-007 has systems: [] in its frontmatter. The step describes confirming that cash collateral has been received and blocked, with an output of Collateral block confirmation recorded. Blocking funds and recording a confirmation would normally be performed in a core banking or trade finance system. No system is associated with this step.

findings.title: DEP-BGID-001 routes Credit System dependency viaSystem TFS (SYS-BGID-002) rather than SYS-BGID-005 directly
findings.detail: DEP-BGID-001 documents the dependency on the Facility/Credit System (SYS-BGID-005), but its frontmatter records viaSystem: [SYS-BGID-002] (Trade Finance System). It is unclear whether the Credit System data flows through TFS as an intermediary (making SYS-BGID-002 correct) or whether SYS-BGID-005 should be listed directly. The architectural routing is unconfirmed.
